  • Hide All Code
  • Download Rmd

Descriptive Stats & Graphics

Summary Statistics

x = rnorm(50) # 50 random values 
#Calculate...
mean(x) # mean
[1] -0.2974185
sd(x) # standard deviation 
[1] 0.9323942
var(x) # variance 
[1] 0.869359
median(x) # median
[1] -0.2689228

Quantiles

Quantiles cut points between equal sized groups.

quantile(x) # quartiles
        0%        25%        50%        75%       100% 
-2.5730602 -0.9634573 -0.2689228  0.3603389  1.3749669 
## computing into other sized groups 
pvec = seq(0, 1, 0.1) #(lower bound, upper bound, step)
quantile(x, pvec)
         0%         10%         20%         30% 
-2.57306022 -1.39619318 -1.15132898 -0.82145166 
        40%         50%         60%         70% 
-0.47161519 -0.26892284  0.01178397  0.24162975 
        80%         90%        100% 
 0.47176626  0.96527645  1.37496689

Examples

library(ISwR)
data("juul")
attach(juul) # specifies where to look 
mean(igf1, na.rm = T) #removes NA values
[1] 340.168
detach(juul)

Summary Function

Calculates multiple summary statistics for a a given vector. Min, Max, missing, Median, Mean and more

summary(juul$igf1)
   Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max.    NA's 
   25.0   202.2   313.5   340.2   462.8   915.0     321

Summary can also run on the whole data set…

summary(juul)
      age            menarche          sex       
 Min.   : 0.170   Min.   :1.000   Min.   :1.000  
 1st Qu.: 9.053   1st Qu.:1.000   1st Qu.:1.000  
 Median :12.560   Median :1.000   Median :2.000  
 Mean   :15.095   Mean   :1.476   Mean   :1.534  
 3rd Qu.:16.855   3rd Qu.:2.000   3rd Qu.:2.000  
 Max.   :83.000   Max.   :2.000   Max.   :2.000  
 NA's   :5        NA's   :635     NA's   :5      
      igf1           tanner        testvol      
 Min.   : 25.0   Min.   :1.00   Min.   : 1.000  
 1st Qu.:202.2   1st Qu.:1.00   1st Qu.: 1.000  
 Median :313.5   Median :2.00   Median : 3.000  
 Mean   :340.2   Mean   :2.64   Mean   : 7.896  
 3rd Qu.:462.8   3rd Qu.:5.00   3rd Qu.:15.000  
 Max.   :915.0   Max.   :5.00   Max.   :30.000  
 NA's   :321     NA's   :240    NA's   :859

But when categorical variables are coded numerically, the data is not necessarily true.

We can correct for this by using ‘factor()’ to convert numbers to a “character” state

juul$sex = factor(juul$sex, labels = c("M","F"))
juul$menarche = factor(juul$menarche, labels = c("No","Yes"))
juul$tanner = factor(juul$tanner, labels = c("I", "II", "III", "IV", "V"))


Graphical Display

For Distributions

hist(x)

hist(x, breaks = 10) # specify the approximate number of bars

Cumulative Distribution

Display the fraction of data that are smaller than or equal to x.

n = length(x)
plot(sort(x), (1:n)/n, type = "s", ylim = c(0,1)) # type can be step or points

x is the kth smallest observation, y is the proportion of k/n of the data is smaller than or equal to x.

Overlaying a Normal Curve on a Histogram

m <- mean(x) 
std <- sd(x)
hist(x, density = 10, breaks = 10, prob = TRUE, # plot hist 
     ylim = c(0,1))               
curve(dnorm(x, mean = m, sd = std), col = "darkblue",
      lwd = 2, add = TRUE)

Q-Q Plot

Normal q-q plots are easity with ‘qqnorm()’

qqnorm(x) #make q-q plot
qqline(x) # add in the line

Increasing the sample size from the normal distribution increases true normality

x = rnorm(500)
qqnorm(x)
qqline(x)

For Grouped Data

data(energy)
expend.lean = energy$expend[energy$stature == "lean"]
expend.obese = energy$expend[energy$stature == "obese"]

Aligning multiple plots by changing default parameters

par(mfrow = c(2,1)) #number of {rows, columns}
hist(expend.lean, breaks = 10, xlim = c(5,13), ylim = c(0,4), col = "white")
Error in plot.new() : figure margins too large

Stripcharts

Valuable for showing distributions withing groups
